description: Weekly performance deep-dive recaps seeded from postmortem analyses

recaps.summary: Valspar Championship — BBI Spearman 0.3994 (DG comparison not computed).
recaps.body_html:
Head-to-Head vs DataGolf

BBI Spearman ρ was 0.3994. DataGolf head-to-head not computed for this event — comparison gap left unfilled in the tracker.

The Winner

Fitzpatrick, Matt won. BBI's pre-tournament rank for the winner is not preserved.

SG Layer Diagnostics

How well each Strokes Gained component correlated with finish position:

  • SG TOTAL: 0.400
  • SG APP: 0.290
  • SG OTT: 0.256
  • SG PUTT: 0.243
  • SG ARG: 0.220

Top-5 Hit Rate

BBI's top-5 pre-tournament picks landed 2 of 5 in the actual top-5. Tough week — narrow distribution.

What's Missing From This Recap

Per-factor attribution (talent vs courseFit vs form vs … contributions to top-10 finishers) was not preserved for Valspar Championship. The bbi_v85 file from this week didn't store the pool_decomp field per player. Forward events (Wk 11 RBC onward) include full attribution.

recaps.summary: Statistical tie with DataGolf — neither model landed a clean win. We ranked Fitzpatrick #3 (tactical win).
recaps.body_html:
Head-to-Head vs DataGolf

This week was a near-tie. BBI's Spearman ρ was 0.3437, while DataGolf logged 0.3770 — a margin of -0.0333 (in DG’s favor). Neither model dominated finishing-order correlation.

This is exactly the kind of week that separates honest forecasting from marketing: we ranked the eventual winner at #3 (DG had #4), which is a tactical win on the individual.

What we're tracking: One event is not a trend. At 5 events in, single-week results move the dial only slightly — the full picture is built over the season.

What BBI Got Right

Winner ranking. Fitzpatrick's pre-tournament profile aligned with BBI's course-fit boost. Ranking them #3 proved sound. Both models identified the archetype; we just ranked them 1 spot higher.

Calibration in Plain English

Brier score measures how far the predicted probability lands from the actual outcome (lower = better). Skill % is the improvement vs. predicting the field-average rate for everyone. For this event:

  • Win market: Brier 0.0203, +14.7% skill — modest edge over naive.
  • Top-5: Brier 0.0551, +18.8% skill — modest edge over naive.
  • Top-10: Brier 0.1008, +13.2% skill — modest edge over naive.
  • Top-20: Brier 0.1911, +7.7% skill — modest edge over naive.
  • Make-cut: Brier 0.0000, +0.0% skill — calibrated, no clear edge.

Building a Multi-Event Track Record

  • Season Spearman (5 events): Currently averaging 0.430. Our 0.40 target is in reach; 0.50 is the best-in-world stretch goal.
  • vs. industry baseline: We're in a +0.037 ahead of the baseline (0.393 baseline mean across 47 events). Some weeks we pull ahead; some we lag — exactly what a young model should look like.
  • Promotion bar: Variant v8.6-cf has met threshold for 5 consecutive weeks — manual review pending. — Heavier course-fit weighting variant. Beats baseline by ≥0.03 Spearman AND ≥5% Brier for 4 consecutive weeks across 15 events.

Next Event

Next up is the Cadillac Championship at Trump National Doral (Blue Monster) (2026-04-30). The pipeline runs Monday — fresh rankings, shadow variants, and market edges land by Tuesday lock.

recaps.summary: Zurich Classic — paused for team-event format. BBI v8.5 is calibrated on individual stroke-play; resumed at Cadillac (Wk 13).
recaps.body_html:
Why This Week Is Skipped

The Zurich Classic of New Orleans runs in 2-player team format (alternate shot R1/R3, best ball R2/R4). BBI v8.5 is built around individual stroke-play — the team scoring structure breaks the math behind every factor (talent normalization, course fit per player, form trends per player).

Per the Team-Event Protocol, the pipeline writes state: "deferred" for the week. PERSEPHONE still tracks live scoring but flags "no BBI baseline." No pool picks. No DFS lineups. No bankroll deployment.

Resume Trigger

Auto-resumed at Wk 13 Cadillac Championship (Trump National Doral, Apr 30–May 3) — first stroke-play event back.

recaps.summary: Statistical tie with DataGolf — neither model landed a clean win. We ranked Reitan #43 (outside our top tier).
recaps.body_html:
Head-to-Head vs DataGolf

This week was a near-tie. BBI's Spearman ρ was 0.1766, while DataGolf logged 0.1449 — a margin of +0.0317 (in our favor). Neither model dominated finishing-order correlation.

This is exactly the kind of week that separates honest forecasting from marketing: we ranked the eventual winner at #43 (DG had #36), which is a miss on the individual but rescued in field-ordering elsewhere.

What we're tracking: One event is not a trend. At 10 events in, single-week results move the dial only slightly — the full picture is built over the season.

What BBI Got Right

Top-20 depth. BBI's top-10 picks landed 9 players in the top-20 (0.45 hit rate) — broad coverage, not just headline picks.

Where BBI Missed

Winner miss. Reitan won outside our top-30 (BBI #43). Worth a courseFit audit to see if this archetype was systematically under-weighted.

Top-5 cashes. Our top-5 produced only 0 top-5 finishes. Suggests over-confidence in mid-tier rankings or rank inversion around positions 5–15.

Field ordering. Spearman ρ 0.1766 sat below the 0.30 floor we expect from a real predictor. Could be small field noise; could be model drift — Mnemosyne flags it for layer attribution review.

Building a Multi-Event Track Record

  • Season Spearman (10 events): Currently averaging 0.346. Our 0.40 target is still ahead; 0.50 is the best-in-world stretch goal.
  • vs. industry baseline: We're in a -0.047 behind the baseline (0.393 baseline mean across 47 events). Some weeks we pull ahead; some we lag — exactly what a young model should look like.
  • Promotion bar: Variant v8.6-cf has met threshold for 5 consecutive weeks — manual review pending. — Heavier course-fit weighting variant. Beats baseline by ≥0.03 Spearman AND ≥5% Brier for 4 consecutive weeks across 15 events.

Next Event

Next up is the PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club (2026-05-14). The pipeline runs Monday — fresh rankings, shadow variants, and market edges land by Tuesday lock.
